response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned senior/staff-level individual contributor with genuine passion for developer tooling and testing infrastructure, but the role is a people-management position requiring at least 2 years of people management and 3 years of formal technical leadership — neither of which appears anywhere in roughly 18 years of work history. His developer-tools experience is largely side projects and open-source work rather than a sustained professional focus, and there is no evidence of setting team priorities, running one-on-ones, performance management, or leading organizational change. Strong IC, wrong role.
strengths: Deep hands-on developer-tooling and testing background (test runners, CI/CD infrastructure, Selenium courses, TDD advocacy)
Broad polyglot engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and low-latency systems at reputable companies (Stripe, Shopify)
Demonstrated mentorship inclination through workshops, courses, and community teaching
concerns: No people-management or team-leadership experience listed, a hard minimum requirement for this role
Developer-tools work is mostly open-source side projects rather than 3+ years in a dedicated professional capacity
No evidence of strategic roadmap ownership, organizational change leadership, or formal technical-lead responsibility

key_factors.factor: No formal technical leadership or roadmap ownership
